page: Amazon Web Services c8gd.2xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARMv9 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 8 dedicated physical cores with one thread per core, 16.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core, and a 474 GB local NVMe SSD. The instance operates on the Nitro hypervisor and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Benchmark data shows average performance across most compute and memory operations. The instance achieves a stress-ng single-core score of 1706.69 and a multi-core score of 13638.03. Memory bandwidth tests show 924.65 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 162.67 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Notably, single-threaded gzip compression is strong at 30.16 GB/sec, placing it in the top 25 percent of tested systems. However, cryptographic performance is a relative weakness, with the PassMark CPU encryption test scoring 1512.16, which falls into the bottom 25 percent.

For machine learning workloads, the instance handles small 135M parameter models with average efficiency, processing 459.53 prompt tokens per second, but struggles with larger 7B models, yielding a poor prompt processing speed of 15.56 tokens per second. Given its compute-to-memory ratio of 1:2 and integrated NVMe storage, this instance offers a resource-dense profile for workloads requiring local scratch space. It is suited for static web serving, software compilation, database operations, and general-purpose ARM64 development.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.2xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server instance powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 16.0 GB of memory, a 474 GB local NVMe SSD, and a 4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance provides dedicated physical cores with a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Benchmarks indicate average performance across standard compute, memory, and database operations, with a strong showing in single-threaded gzip compression at 30.16 GB/sec. However, it exhibits weaker performance in cryptographic encryption and larger LLM prompt processing. This instance is suited for workloads requiring local high-speed storage and dedicated ARM compute resources, such as software compilation, static web serving, and database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.2xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring 8 Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 16 GB RAM, and a 474 GB local NVMe SSD, delivering balanced compute and storage performance.

tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated Graviton cores, integrated NVMe storage, and balanced performance for localized workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARMv9 processor at 2.8 GHz
Includes 16.0 GB of memory and a 474 GB local NVMe SSD
Delivers strong single-threaded gzip compression performance of 30.16 GB/sec
Features a 4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Suited for software compilation, static web serving, and database workloads
